steps.title: Spot the comma introducing an absolute phrase
steps.content: After “artistry,” the comma sets off “its vehicles [blank] by collectors...”—a noun phrase standing independently and modifying the main clause. This is an absolute phrase: a noun + participle construction that provides additional information without becoming part of the main verb.

steps.title: Match the participle form for absolute phrases
steps.content: ”Highly prized” is a past participle used as an adjective—exactly what absolute phrases require. Choices B, C, and D use full verbs (“had been,” “are,” “were”) that would create a complete clause instead of the reduced absolute phrase structure needed here. This matches choice A — A is correct.
